title: Family members of H1-B visa holder applicants - Canada.ca
content:
Family members of H-1B visa holder applicants who’ve already applied may be eligible to apply for any of the
    following:
- temporary resident visa (TRV)
To qualify, you must
- be the spouse , common-law
            partner or dependent
            child of an H-1B visa holder applicant
- currently live in the US
- apply on or before September 26, 2024
If you’re not eligible to come to Canada under this policy, you may qualify as a family member of a highly skilled worker .

## How to apply for a TRV or study permit
- To apply for a visitor visa or study permit as a family member of an H­1B applicant who’s already applied, you
            must apply through the IRCC Portal .
- Follow the general steps to apply for a visitor
                    visa study
                    permit
- visitor
                    visa
- study
                    permit
When you apply, answer the following question exactly as shown . Otherwise
- we won't be able to confirm that you’re applying as a family member of an H-1B visa applicant who’s already
        applied and
- your application may be delayed or refused
“Tell us more about what you’ll do in Canada”

## How to apply for an open work permit
Follow the steps below to apply for an open work permit as a family member of an H-1B visa applicant who’s already
    applied.

### Step 1: Get your documents ready
To apply for an open work permit as a family member, you'll need
- the Canadian work permit application number of the original H-1B applicant
- proof that you're the spouse, common-law partner or dependent child of an H-1B visa holder who has applied under
        this policy, such as your marriage certificate or Statutory Declaration of Common-Law Union (IMM 5409) if you are a spouse or
                common-law partner your birth certificate or adoption papers, if you are a dependent child
- your marriage certificate or Statutory Declaration of Common-Law Union (IMM 5409) if you are a spouse or
                common-law partner
- your birth certificate or adoption papers, if you are a dependent child
- proof that you live in the US , such as a completed Form I-94, Arrival/Departure Record a recent utility bill an income tax report any document that proves you live in the US
- a completed Form I-94, Arrival/Departure Record
- a recent utility bill
- an income tax report
- any document that proves you live in the US

### Step 2: Check if you need a medical exam
Find
        out if you need a medical exam for your application. Most people don't need a medical exam to enter Canada
    through this measure.

### If you need a medical exam
- You should find a doctor and get
            an upfront medical exam before applying.
- You'll get an open work permit with no medical restrictions.

### If you don't need a medical exam
- You'll get an open work permit with limits on where you can work.

### Step 3: Apply online through the IRCC Portal
If you're applying as a family member for an open work permit under this temporary policy, follow these
    instructions.

### If the IRCC portal requests your UCI
If you’re applying for the first time, you won’t have a Unique Client Identifier (UCI) yet. Enter 00000000 if the portal asks for your UCI. You can then continue with your application.
- Make a new account if it's your first
            time using the IRCC Portal . Once you enter your email address, we'll send you a code to sign up.
- Once you enter your email address, we'll send you a code to sign up.
- After you log in: You must read and agree to the terms and conditions to continue with the application. Click the Start application button. Click Apply to visit, study or work in Canada as a temporary resident . On the next page, check the box at the bottom to confirm that you have read and understand the
                information shown, then click Start application . If you're applying to come to Canada with other eligible family members, answer the following
                    questions: Question: Do you want to apply for more than 1 person at the same time? Answer: Yes Question: What type of group applies to your situation? Answer: Select "Family." On the Manage group page, click Add member for each member of
                        your family. You must complete an application for each family member, but you
                            can submit them all at
                            once when you're done. Question: I want to apply for a ? Answer: work permit Question: What exactly do you want to apply for? Answer: I am the spouse, common law partner or dependant of an H-1B
                                visa holder. Continue answering the remaining questions to complete your application.
- You must read and agree to the terms and conditions to continue with the application.
- Click the Start application button.
- Click Apply to visit, study or work in Canada as a temporary resident .
- On the next page, check the box at the bottom to confirm that you have read and understand the
                information shown, then click Start application .
- If you're applying to come to Canada with other eligible family members, answer the following
                    questions: Question: Do you want to apply for more than 1 person at the same time? Answer: Yes Question: What type of group applies to your situation? Answer: Select "Family." On the Manage group page, click Add member for each member of
                        your family. You must complete an application for each family member, but you
                            can submit them all at
                            once when you're done. Question: I want to apply for a ? Answer: work permit Question: What exactly do you want to apply for? Answer: I am the spouse, common law partner or dependant of an H-1B
                                visa holder.
- Question: Do you want to apply for more than 1 person at the same time? Answer: Yes
- Question: What type of group applies to your situation? Answer: Select "Family."
- Answer: Select "Family."
- On the Manage group page, click Add member for each member of
                        your family.
- You must complete an application for each family member, but you
                            can submit them all at
                            once when you're done.
- Question: I want to apply for a ? Answer: work permit
- Question: What exactly do you want to apply for? Answer: I am the spouse, common law partner or dependant of an H-1B
                                visa holder.
- Answer: I am the spouse, common law partner or dependant of an H-1B
                                visa holder.
- Continue answering the remaining questions to complete your application.

## Follow the application instructions
You must follow these instructions exactly so we can identify your application under this
        temporary policy. Otherwise
- we won’t be able to confirm that you’re applying as a family member of an H-1B visa holder that has applied and
- your application may be delayed or refused
Start your application

### Who's eligible to apply on paper
You’re only eligible to apply for this policy on paper if you have a disability that prevents you from applying
        online.
If your open work permit was issued with a validity period of less than 3 years, you may be eligible for an
    extension.
Find out more about open work permit extensions for family members of H-1B visa holders .
